line: Methodist Episcopal Church.

The society of the M. E. Church, in this place, was organized in the year 1846. The church was built in the year 1848--enlarged or re-built 1858-- present dimensions 58 feet long by 26 feet wide, of a good proportion in height. The society at present consists in a membership of about one hundred. Service every Sabbath, as follows, viz: Preaching at 10½ A. M., and at 7½ P. M.; Class meeting at 12 M.; Sun- day School at 1½ P. M. Meetings during page the week as follows, viz: Class meet- ing on Tuesday evening, Prayer meeting on Thursday evening, meeting of the Choir on Saturday evening. Number in Sunday School about one hundred and twenty.
First Baptist Church
The first Baptist Church of Moline was organized June 5th, 1852--eighteen persons uniting in the organization. Their first pastor was Rev. A. G. Eber- hart, who commenced his labors August
ionary of State Association, and
YOUNG MEN'S ASSOCIATION.
This Association was founded
Debating Society and the sustaining

tures. There is now connected with it a literary paper called the "OLIO," made up from original contributions by the ladies of Moline, and which is read at each alternate meeting of the Associa- tion, forming a prominent and interest- ing feature of its exercises. The Li- brary now numbers about 250 volumes, and to the increasing of which all the surplus funds of the society are applied. The Association has about sixty acting members, and is in a flourishing condi- tion. It meets every Monday evening, at Chamberlin's Hall, from the 1st of October until the 1st of March, and the first Monday evening of each month dur- ing the remainder of the year. All meetings public.

FIRST METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH. Organized in 1833. Situated at the northeast corner Fifth and Brady streets. Has 290 members, Rev. Charles G. Trnsdell Pastor. Hours of service, 10 o'clock A. M. and 74 P. M. in summer, and 11 A. M. and 7 P. M. in winter.
ST. ANTHONY'S (CATHOLIC) CHURCH. Organized in 1838. Situated at the northeast corner of Fourth and Main streets, Rev. J. A. M. Pelamorgues, Priest. Full information not given.

FIRST PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH, (O. S.) -Organized May 5th, 1839. Situated on the north side of Third street, between Main and Harrison. Has 190 members, Rev. McC. Anderson, Pas- tor. Hours of service, 10 o'clock A. M. and 74 P. M. in summer, and 11 A. M. and 74 P. M. in winter.

CHRISTIAN CHURCH, OR DISCIPLES. Organized July 28, 1839. Situated at the east side of Brady, between Fourth and Fifth streets. Has 220 members, Elder James Challen Pastor. Hours of service, 10 o'clock A. M., and 74 P. M. Meetings for social worship every Thursday evening, in the lecture room.

DAVENPORT CONGREGATIONAL CHURCH. Organized July 30th, 1839; re-organized as the Edwards Congregational Church August 17th, 1861. Situated at the north side of Fifth street, between Brady and Main. Has 47 members, Rev. Wm. Windsor Pastor. Hours of service, 101 o'clock A. M, all the year, and 74 P. M. in summer, and 7 in winter.

organization_name: FIRST, OR MAIN STREET BAPTIST CHURCH
line: FIRST, OR MAIN STREET BAPTIST CHURCH. Organized in 1839; re-organized in 1860. Situated at the northwest corner of Main and. Sixth streets, Has 40 members, Rev. Thomas Powell home supply. Services at 10 A. M.

TRINITY CHURCH, (Protestant Episcopal.) - Organized Nov. 4th, 1841. Situated at the southeast corner of Fifth and Rock Island streets. Has 131 members, Rev. F. Emerson Judd, Rector. Hours of service, 10 o'clock A. M., and 74 P. M.

SECOND BAPTIST CHURCH. Organized October 7th, 1851. Situated at the southwest corner of Fourth and Perry streets. Has 150 members, Rev. Isaac Butterfield Pastor. Hours of service, 101 o'clock A. M. and 74 P. M. in summer, and 11 A. M. and 7 P. M. in winter:
UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH. Organized as the Asso- ciate Reformed Presbyterian Church in 1854. Situated at the
ST. KUNIGUNDI'S (GERMAN CATHOLIC) CHURCH. Organized in 1855. Situated at the east side of Marquette, between Fifth and Sixth streets; Rev. Anton Niermann, Priest. Full information not given.

ST. LUKE'S PARISH, (PROTESTANT EPISCOPAL).-Organ- ized April 4, 1856. Situated at the north-west corner 7th and Brady streets. Has 68 communicants. Rev. Horatio N. Powers, Rector. Hoars of service, 10 o'clock A. M. through- out the year. Evening service 7 o'clock in winter and 74 in summer.

ST. MARGUERITE'S CHURCH, (CATHOLIC). -Organized in October, 1856. Is situated at the north-west corner of Tenth and LeClaire streets. Has 1200 members. Rev. Henry Cos- grove, Priest. Hours of service, 7 and 10 o'clock A. M. and 3 P. M.

GERMAN CONGREGATIONAL CHURCH. Organized July 19, 1857. Situated south side of Fifth street, between Warren and Vine. Has 28 members. Rev. Henry Langpaap, Pastor. Hours of service, 104 A. M. and 7+ P. M.
GERMAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH. Organized May 14, 1859, north-east corner of Sixth and Warren streets. Has 48 members. J. M. Winkler, Pastor. Hours of service, 10 o'clock A M and 74 P M.

REFORMED PROTESTANT DUTCH CHURCH. Organized in October, 1859. Situated at the corner of 11th and Brady streets. Has 42 members. Rev. Cyrus G. Van Derveer, Pastor. Hours of service 10 o'clock A M and 74 P M.

GERMAN EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH.-Organized October, 1861. Situated at the south-east corner of 8th and Gaines streets. Has 45 members. Rev. John G. Kiesel, Pastor. Hours of service, 10 o'clock A. M.

Methodist Episcopal Sabbath School. Organized in 1836. Held in the basement of the M. E. Church, south-east corner of Fifth and Brady streets. Has 15-teachers and about 150 scholars. O. W. Skinner, Superintendent. Hour of meet- ing, 9 o'clock A. M. in summer and 94 in winter.
St. Anthony's (Catholic) Sunday School. -Meets in the Church every Sunday afternoon. Full information not given. Probably organized in 1838.

Davenport Congregational Sabbath School. Organized in 1839, re-organized as the Sabbath School of the Edwards Congregational Church, Aug., 1861. Meets in the Church. Has 14 teachers and 100 scholars. Edward Russell, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 2 o'clock P. M.
Triaity Sabbath School, (Protestant Episcopal.) -Organized in 1841. Meets in Trinity Church. Has 20 teachers and 123 scholars. John E. Henry, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 3 o'clock P. M.

Presbyterian Sabbath School. Organized March 6, 1846. Meets in the lecture room of the Church, north side of Third street between Main and Harrison. Has 21 teachers and 125 scholars. James M. Dalzell, Superintendent. Hour of meət- ing, 9 o'clock A. M. in summer and 94 in winter.
Second Baptist Sunday School. Organized in 1851. Meets in basement of Second Baptist Church. Has 18 teachers and 125 scholars, S. F. Smith, Superintendent. Hour of meet- ing, 9 o'clock A. M.

Christian Church Sunday School. Organized January 6th, 1852. Meets in the Christian Chapel. Has 12 teachers and 100 scholars. Elder James Challen superintends the school. Hour of meettng, 9 o'clock A. M.

English Evangelical Lutheran Sunday School. Organized in March, 1855, Meets in the German Evangelical Lutheran Church. Has 10 teachers and 50 scholars. Elias Shull, Su- perintendent. Hour of meeting, 9 o'clock A. Μ.
St Kunigandi's (German Catholic) Sunday School.-Informa- tion not given. Probably organised in 1855.

St. Luke's (Episcopal) Sunday School. -Organized in 1856. Meets in the basement of St. Luke's Church, north west cor- ner 7th and Brady streets. Has 12 teachers and 85 scholars. Charles Stewart, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 2 o'clock P. M.

organization_name: Sabbath School of the United Presbyterian Church
line: Sabbath School of the United Presbyterian Church-Organ- ized January, 1856. Meets in the Church. Has 13 teachers and 75 scholars. J. W. Jamison, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 1 o'clock P. M.

German Congregational Sunday School. Organized in 1857. Is held in the German Congregational Church, south side of Fifth street between Warren and Vine. Has 4 teachers and from 25 to 80 scholars. Henry Dore, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 9 o'clock A. M.
St. Marguerite's (Catholic) Sunday School. Organized in
1859. Is held in St. Marguerite's Church. Has 10 teachers and 200 scholars. Conducted by the Rev. Henry Cosgrove. Hour of meeting, 2 o'clock P. M.

German Methodist Episcopal Sabbath School.-Organized in 1859. Held in the German M. E. Church. Has 12 teachers and from 40 to 50 scholars. A. Peterson, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 9 o'clock A. M.

organization_name: First or Main Street Baptist Sunday School
line: First or Main Street Baptist Sunday School. Organized October, 1860. Meets in Main Street Baptist Church. Has 7 teachers and 45 scholars. Rev. Thomas Powell, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 9 o'clock A. M.

organization_name: Sunday School of the Reformed Protestant Dutch Church
line: Sunday School of the Reformed Protestant Dutch Church.- Organized in July, 1861. Meets in the Church. Has 20 teachers and 90 scholars. L. S. Viele, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 3 o'clock P. M.

East Davenport Mission Sunday School. Was organized in 1855. It meets in the district school house, East Davenport. Is conducted on the Union plan, and has 8 teachers and 85 scholars. Charles H. Kent is the Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 2 o'clock P. M.

organization_name: Mission Sunday School of the First Baptist Church
line: Mission Sunday School of the First Baptist Church. Was organized in 1856, by Dr. Witherwax, at a private residence, and after it increased in numbers, a larger room was rented, and finally, by the continued exertions of Dr. W., a one-story frame building was erected for the use of the school on Fourth street near Gaines, where it is now held. It has 9 teachers and 70 scholars. Dr. C. G. Blood, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 1½ o'clock P. M.

West Davenport Mission Sunday School. Organized in 1859. Meets in the district (stone) school house, on Second street near the western city limits. Has 10 teachers and 68 scholars. E. A. Lynde, Superintendent. Hour of meeting, 1½ o'clock P. М.

organization_name: Scott Division No. 1, Sons of Temperance
line: Scott Division No. 1, Sons of Temperance. Organized October 5, 1847. Meets every Friday evening in Temper- ance Hall, second story of new State Bank building, south- east corner of Third and Brady streets. Present officers, Charles E. Putnam, W. P.; John E. Henry, W. A.; Franklin Kirk, R. S.; J. Smith Conner, F. S.; Jolin Collins, Treas. It is not easy to ascertain the number of its members; so many having entered the army, but as an example of its efficiency, the roll of membership shows that for the first six months of the current year, 350 have been initiated. Many of them from the camps in and around the city were thus, at the same time, drilling to fight king Alcohol and king Jeff. The fires have never ceased to burn on the altars of this Division from its organization. It has not at any time missed a single meeting for the want of a quorum.

organization_name: Adar Lodge No. 257, Independent Order of Good Tem- plars
line: Adar Lodge No. 257, Independent Order of Good Tem- plars. Organized July 13, 1861. Meets every Tuesday evening in Temperance Hall. Present officers, J. J. Sev- erance, W. C. T.; Mrs. C. B. McLaughlin, W. V. T.; Mrs. Anna Collins, W. S.; Miss Fanny Rouser, Treas. Has 70 members.

Davenport Ladies' Soldiers' Aid Society. Organized September, 1861. Meets every Tuesday afternoon in the basement of the Christian Church, east side of Brady be- tween Fourth and Fifth streets. Present officers, Mrs. D. T. Newcomb, President; Mrs. Alfred Sanders, Vice Presi- dent; Mrs. Capt. John Galligan, Treasurer; Mrs. Charles Lesslie, Secretary. The real workers of this Society are few, but they compensate by their industry for the lack of numbers, and have done much in the preparation of delica- cies to relieve the sufferings of our soldiers in the field.

Davenport City Relief Society. Organized August 28th, 1863, for the purpose of giving aid to the destitute in the city. Officers: Mayor John E. Henry, President; George L. Davenport, Treasurer; F. H. Griggs, Secretary.
Davenport Lodge, No. 37. Stated meetings first Monday before the full moon in each month, in Masonic Hall, Griggs' Block, east side Perry, between Second and Third streets.
Davenport Royal Arch Chapter, No. 16. Stated meetings second Tuesday in each month. Officers: O. H. Watson, H. P.; A. P. Luse, Sec'y; E Y. Lane, Treas. Meets in Masonic Hall.

Davenport Lodge, No. 7.-Mcets every Tuesday evening, in Odd Fellows' Hall, west side of Brady, between Fifth and Sixth streets. Richard Hood, N. G.; J. C. Teagarden, V. G.; John M. Lyter, R. S.; John Yantis, P. S.; Israel Hall, Treas.

Twin City Degree Lodge, No. 1.-Instituted March 8th, 1856. Meets first Friday in each month, in Odd Fellows' Hall.

FIRST METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH. Organized in 1836.- Situated on the northeast corner Fifth and Brady streets. Rev. H. Bailies Pastor. Hour of service 10 o'clock, A. M., and 7 P. M. in Summer, and 11 A. M. and 7 P. M. in winter.

CHRISTIAN CHURCH, OR DISCIPLES. Organized 1839. Situated at the east side of Brady between Fourth and Fifth streets. Elder James Challen, Pastor.
EDWARDS CONGREGATIONAL CHURCH. Organized 1839. Situated at the north side of Fifth street, between Brady and Main. Rev. Wm. Windsor, Pastor.
TRINITY CHURCH, (PROTESTANT EPISCOPAL.) - Organized 1841. Situated at the southeast corner of Fifth and Rock Island sts. Rev. Alfred Stubbs, Rector.
CALVERY BAPTIST CHURCH. Organized 1851. Situated at the southwest corner of Fourth and Perry streets. Rev. S. D. Watson, Pastor.
UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH. Organized 1854. Situated at the corner of Eleventh and Scott streets. Rev. Henry Wallace, Pastor.
ST. KUNIGUNDI'S CHURCH, (German Catholic.)-Organized 1855. Situated at the east side of Marquette, between Fifth and Sixth streets. Rev. Anton Niermann, Priest.
ST. MARGUERITE'S CHURCH, (Catholic). -Organized 1856. Situated at the northwest corner of Tenth and Le Claire sts. Rev. Henry Cosgrove, Priest.
GERMAN CONGREGATIONAL CHURCH. Organized 1857. Situated south side of Fifth, between Warren and Vine. Rev. I. J. Graf, Pastor.
GERMAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH. Organized 1859.- Situated on northeast corner Fifth and Warren streets. Rev. Chas Holtkamp, Pastor.
REFORMED PROTESTANT DUTCH CHURCH. Organized 1859. Situated at the corner of Eleventh and Brady sts. Rev. Cyrus G. Van Derveer, Pastor.

CALVARY BAPTIST CHURCH.- Organized, October 6, 1851. Church, South-west corner of Fourth and Perry streets. Number of members, 315. Meet- ings: Sunday, in Winter, 11 A. M., and 7 P. M.; in Summer, 101 A. M., and 71 P. M. Communion, first Sunday in each month. Covenant meeting, Thursday evening preceding Communion. Prayer meetings: Young peoples', Tuesday evening; General prayer meeting, Thursday evening. Officers: Pastor, Rev. D. S. Watson; Deacons: H. Alling, J. Eldridge, I. N. Field, C. G. Blood, (office of Deacons permanent); Organist, A. W. Blakely; Chorister, D. E. Taylor; Sexton, J. C. Landt; Board of Trustees, elected annually on the first Monday in January: J. T. Lane, C. G. Blood, L. Davis; Clerk, D. H. Hartwell; Treas, H. Williams. Annual meeting for the renting of pews, first Monday in May. Sunday School: Meets, in Winter at 91 A. M., and in Summer at 9 A. M.; Superintendent, W. L. Carroll; Assistant, A. J. Monta- gue; Librarian, L. B. Tichnor; Sec., M. D. Snyder; Treas., J. W.. Ballard. Officers elected first week in January. Number of teachers, 22; Number of scholars, 175; Number of papers, 125 copies weekly.

GERMAN BAPTIST MISSION.- Organized, June 1, 1866. Chapel, north-west corner Third and Myrtle streets. Meetings: Sermon, in Winter, 104 A. M., and 7 P. M.; Summer, 101 A. M., and 74 P. M. Communion, last Sun- day in each month. Covenant meeting, Wednesday evening preceding Communion. Prayer meeting, Wednesday evenings, at 7 o'clock. Bible Class, meets Friday evening at 7 o'clock. Pastor, Rev. E. J. Deckmann. Deacon, C. Landt. Sunday School: Meets, at 9 A. M.; Superintendent, Rev. E. J. Deckmann; Number of teachers, 10; Number of scholars, 90.

CHRISTIAN CHURCH.- Organized, July 28, 1839. Chapel, east side Brady street, be- tween Fourth and Fifth. Present number of members, 225. Officers: Pastor, Rev. J. Challen; Elders, Rev. J. Challen, R. S.

THE EDWARDS CONGREGATIONAL CHURCH.- Organized August 17, 1861. Number of members, 75. Church, north-east corner of Fifth and Main streets. Officers: Pastorate vacant; Deacons, elected for three years, E. Russell, W. A. Be- mis, S. Roberts; Treasurer, J. Goldsbury. Service: Sunday morning, at 101 o'clock, and on each Sunday evening, during the winter, at 7 o'clock, and on each Sunday evening, during the summer, at 7 o'clock. Regular weekly prayer meeting, every Thursday evening, in the Lecture room, at 7 o'clock in winter and 8 o'clock in summer. Sabbath School: Superintendent, E. Russell; Asst. Superintendents, J. S. Conner and Miss M. Tripp; Secretary, J. J. Nagle; Librarian, Miss M. L. Davies; Asst. Li- brarians, J. F. Baker and C. Sleeper; Treasurer, J. Goldsbury; Number of teachers, 21; Average attendance of scholars for year 1866, 180; Total enrollment, 200 scholars; Number of volumes in library for 1867, 500; Number of weekly papers, for free dis- tribution to school, for year 1867, 200; School meets every Sun- day afternoon at 2 o'clock; Youths' Missionary Society meets on second Sunday of each month, at 3 o'clock, P. M.; Teachers' prayer meeting on the third Sunday of each month, at 34 o'clock, P. M.; Anniversary meeting and festival, on each New Year's day, at 2 o'clock, P. M.; All officers of school and missionary so- ciety elected annually. The Edwards Congregational Society, incorporated under the laws of the State of Iowa, November 19,
LUSE & GRIGGS, Stationers and Booksellers, 55 Perry Street.

GERMAN CONGREGATIONAL CHURCH.- Organized, July 19, 1857. Situated, south side of Fifth street, between Warren and Vine. Number of members, 38; Meetings: Sermon, in Winter, 104 A. M., and 7 P. M.; in Summer, 10 A. M., and 8 P. M. Communion, three times every year. Prayer meet- ing, Wednesday evening, 7 o'clock. Officers: Pastor, Rev. J. F. Graf; Deacons, elected in June for one year, M. N. Cornelius, J. M. Paustian. Library of the Church contains 140 volumes. Li- brarians; C. Schnoor, H. F. Dose. Sunday School: Meets, in Summer and Winter, 94 A. M.; Superintendent, J. C. Hoffmann; Number of teachers, 9; Number of scholars, about 100.

TRINITY CHURCH- Episcopal. Organized, 1841. Church, south-east corner of Fifth and Rock Island streets. Officers: Rector, Rev. A. H. Stubbs; Wardens, J. H. Berryhill, W. F. Ross; Vestry, E. Cook, J. E. Henry, S. E. Brown, W. A. Remington, C. S. Whisler, A. Kimball; Treasurer, S. E. Brown; Clerk, D. B. Shelley.
THE BISHOP'S CHURCH.- Situated on the north side of Eleventh street, between Harri son and Main. Rt. Rev. H. W. Lee, D. D., Rector; Rev. H. N. Powers and Rev. H. Townsend, assistants.

FIRST GERMAN EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH.- Organized, June 8, 1862. Church, Gaines street, south east corner of Eighth. Number of members, 45. Meetings: Sermon. in Winter, 101 A. M, and irregularly in the evening; in Summer, 10 A. M. Communion: Christmas, Easter-day Whitsunday and month of October. Covenant meeting, preceding Communion. Officers: Pastor, Rev. J. Ritter; Church Counsels, elected New Year's day, for one and two years, Elder, J. Hiller, sr.; Deacons, B. H. Borgelt, J. Flamboe; Trustees, G. P. Crecelius, P. Cramer. Organist, F. Hass. Sunday School: Meets, in Winter, at 2 P. M. and in Summer, at 9 A. M.; Superintendent, Rev. J. Ritter, Number of teachers, 3; Number of scholars, 30; Number of vol umes, in German, 150, in English, 300; No. of papers, 300 copies.

METHODIST EPISCOPAL CHURCH.- Organized January 1, 1842. Church north-east corner of Fifth and Brady sts. Number of members, 340. Meetings: Sermon, Winter, 11 A. M., and 7 P. M.; in Summer, 101 A. M., and 74 P.M. Childrens meeting in the vestry every Saturday at 2 P. M. Prayer meeting Thursday evening. Young men's prayer meeting, Saturday evening. Class meetings, Sunday 9 A. M., 12 M., and 3 P. M.; also, Tuesday and Wednesday evenings. Offi- cers: Pastor, Rev. A. B. Kendig; Presiding Elder of the District, R. W. Keeler, A. M.; Superanuated preachers, J. Gilruth, H. Baylies. Local Elders: G. Crimp, W. O. Hiskey. Local Dea cons: J. G. G. Cavendish, A. G. Fletcher. Trustees: H. Price, W. L. Cook, Prest., P. H. Worley, J. Bradshaw, H. Hills, K. Reading, J. Hornby, W. Hender, Treas., T. K. Fluke. Stew- ards: H. Price, C. S. Streeper, Treas., S. L. Mitchell, W. F. Fid- lar, J. Annable, sr., W. L. Cook, D. B. Shaw, R. J. Watts, W. Ade.

FIRST PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH.- Organized May 5, 1839. Edifice north-west corner of Brady and Seventh sts. Number of members, 160. Sermon in Summer, 10 A. M. and 7 P. M.; in Winter, 11 A. M. and 7 P. M. Communion, the second Sabbath of January, and quarterly thereafter. Preparatory lecture at 3 P. M. of the preceding Saturday. Prayer meeting on Thursday evening. Pastor, S. McAnderson. Elders (or members of Session,) J. MeD. Bur rows, C. E. Kinkead, S. J. Sanger, O. A. Barker, C. Thorpe. Treasurer, W. H. Farrand. Trustees: C. E. Putnam, C. E. Kin- kead, S. J. Sanger, J. H. Farrand, C. S. Ells. Bible Class, meets with the Sabbath School; teacher, J. T. Walker. Sabbath Scoool meets 9 A. M. in Summer, 94 in Winter; S. J. Sanger, Superin- tendent, J. Altman, Asst.; W. H. Farrand, librarian; Mrs. R. Hake, teacher infant School.

UNITED PRESBYTERIAN CHURCH.- Organized as the Associate Reformed Presbyterian Church in 1854. Situated on the corner of Eleventh and Scott sts. Num- ber of members, 45. Meetings: Sermon in Winter, 2 P. M.; in Summer, 3 P. M. Prayer-Meetings, 7 P. M., on Sabbath and Thursday evenings. Officers: Pastor, Rev. H. Wallace. Elders A. Blair, J. M. May, M. G. Hamill, elected for life. Trustees: J. W. Jamison, Treas.; J. Donahue, N. J. McKelvey, J. M. May, elected annually. Sabbath School: in Winter at 34 P. M., in Summer, 11 P. M.; Superintendent, J. K. McCosh, Asst. Supt, J. W. Jamison. Secretary, Miss E. Johnston. Librarian, J. Donahue. Treasurer, Mrs. G. McConnel. Number of teachers, 9; pupils, 50; number volumes in library, 220; papers, 50 copies.

Reformed Protestant Dutch Church. Organized, October 29, 1859. Church, Brady street, north- east corner Eleventh. Meetings: On the Sabbath, in Winter, 11 A. M., and 7 P. M.; in Summer, 104 A. M., and 7 P. M. Com munion, first Sabbath in February, May, August and November. Prayer meeting. Thursday evening. Officers: Elders, J. N. Rog ers, J. G. Crane; Deacons, T. Crimp, J. N. Crawford; Trustees, J. N. Crawford, J. L. Swits, D. Washburn; Treasurer, J. L Swits. Sunday School: Meets, in Winter, at 21 P. M., and in Sum- mer, at 3 P. M.; Superintendent, L. S. Viele; Librarian, S. B. Reed.

ST. ANTHONY'S CHURCH.- Main street north-east corner Fourth. Organized in the fall of 1839 by the Very Rev. J. A. M. Pelamourgues, who has been the pastor of the congregation ever since. Number of members, about 2,000. Meetings at 71 and 10 A. M., and at 7 P. M. The Sodality for children meets at 24 P. M., and that for young ladies at 34 P. M. The library for children has 327 volumes.
ST. KUNIGUNDA'S CHURCH, (GERMAN.)-- Organized, 1855. Church, Marquette street, north-east corner of Sixth. Pastor, Rev. A. Niermann.

ST. MARGUERITE'S CHURCH. - Organized October 1856. Situated on the north-west corner of Tenth and LeClaire sts. Number of members, 1,800. Hours of service, 71 and 101 A. M., and 34 P. M. Pastor Rev. H. Cos- grove; Asst., Rev. P. F. Harrison. Sunday School at 24 P. M.

line: EAST DAVENPORT UNION MISSION SABBATH SCHOOL.- Organized 1854. Building, Main street, north side, west of Mound, East Davenport. Meets at 2 o'clock P. M. Concerts monthly at same hour. C. H. Kent, Supt., W. A. Bemis, Asst. Supt. and Secretary; W. L. Carroll, Treasurer, Miss A. Ahrens, Librarian. Library Committee, Miss S. E. Squires, Miss C. Park- hurst, Mr. Page. Gould, Treas.; F. W. Flower, Sec. Directors: J. T. Lane, N. Kuhnen, F. W. Flower, A. P. Luse, H. II. Andressen, H. Lischer, D. Gould.

line: LIBERTY STEAM FIRE ENGINE COMPANY, No. 1.- Organized, March 5, 1866, (formerly Pilot Fire Engine and Hose Company, organized May, 1864.) Engine house, north-east corner of Second and Brown streets. Meets, the first Monday in each month. Number of members, 80 active, 18 honorary. Officers, elected at regular meeting in January, for one year: President, J. F. Miller; Foreman, J. Dott; First Asst., J. Pries- ter; Second Asst., B. Schoo; Engineer, M. Donahue; Firemen, J. Pargue, A. Schaefer; Treasurer, F. Bilz; Secretary, F. Voss. The Company have one third class Button steamer, one four- wheel hose carriage and 1,000 feet of serviceable leather hose.
